grounding_note: Expansion batch: more charging-document disclosures behind conduct narratives.

scenarios.opening_query: My husband was arrested after our neighbor's garage caught fire during his bonfire. It was an accident.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony
scenarios.hidden_fact: The DA charged felony Reckless Burning because the damage topped $40,000.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They filed felony reckless burning — the damage was over $40,000.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: the charged offense level and damage valuation
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a misdemeanor reckless burning citation — the fire scorched some siding, maybe $800.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a misdemeanor citation — about $800 of scorched siding.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm charged over a fight at my kid's hockey game that the other guy started on video.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Lesser Felony || Criminal Law > Misdemeanor
scenarios.hidden_fact: The complaint charges Harassment and Disorderly Conduct II — both misdemeanors, no injury alleged.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The complaint says harassment and disorderly conduct — both misdemeanors, no injury.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They filed Assault III as a felony because he says his orbital bone cracked.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's felony Assault III — he claims a cracked orbital bone.
scenarios.vetting_note: Video-vindication feeling is irrelevant to routing; the counts are.

scenarios.opening_query: My boyfriend got arrested with my car and now I have charges too. I didn't do anything.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony || Criminal Law > Major Felony
scenarios.hidden_fact: They charged me with delivery of a controlled substance too, saying the amount in the trunk makes me part of it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They charged me with delivery too — saying the amount in the trunk makes me part of it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: what the passenger/owner is actually charged with
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: My charge is misdemeanor Hindering — they say I lied about who was driving.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Mine's misdemeanor hindering — they say I lied about who drove.
scenarios.vetting_note: Constructive-possession stakes; her own count is the routing fact.

scenarios.opening_query: I got charged after defending myself outside my apartment when a drunk guy came at me.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony
scenarios.hidden_fact: Because I had my pocketknife out, they filed Unlawful Use of a Weapon — a felony — on top of the assault count.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Because my pocketknife was out, there's a felony weapon count on top of the assault charge.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a weapon count elevates the case
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's one count of Assault IV, misdemeanor — no weapon was involved on my side.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: One misdemeanor Assault IV count — no weapon on my side.
scenarios.vetting_note: Self-defense narrative constant; weapon element does the flipping.

scenarios.opening_query: The police cited me over expired tags and it somehow turned into a whole criminal thing.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Traffic Offenses || Criminal Law > Misdemeanor
scenarios.hidden_fact: During the stop they charged me with misdemeanor Driving While Suspended — I didn't know my license had been suspended over an unpaid ticket.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They added misdemeanor driving-while-suspended — I had no idea an unpaid ticket suspended me.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a criminal charge was added to the violation
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It stayed a violation — tags and no proof of insurance; I want to contest the insurance one.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Just violations — tags and the insurance one I want to contest.
scenarios.vetting_note: Ticket-to-crime escalation is common and users under-describe it.

scenarios.opening_query: My nephew is facing serious charges from a house party where things got stolen.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Lesser Felony || Criminal Law > Juvenile Offender || Criminal Law > Misdemeanor
scenarios.hidden_fact: He's 15 — the case is in juvenile court, and the county wants him in a diversion program if he admits it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's 15 — juvenile court, and they're offering diversion if he admits it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: the accused's age and court system
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's 19 — adult court, charged with felony Theft I for the electronics.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He's 19 — adult court, felony Theft I over the electronics.
scenarios.vetting_note: Age fact selects an entire court system; 'nephew' hides it naturally.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm being prosecuted over money from my grandmother's accounts that I was helping her manage.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony || Criminal Law > Major Felony
scenarios.hidden_fact: The indictment charges first-degree Criminal Mistreatment and Aggravated Theft — they total it at $180,000 over four years.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The indictment says first-degree criminal mistreatment and aggravated theft — $180,000 over four years, they claim.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: the indicted counts and alleged amount
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's one misdemeanor count over a single $400 withdrawal they say wasn't authorized.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: One misdemeanor count — a single $400 withdrawal they call unauthorized.
scenarios.vetting_note: Elder-finance prosecutions span the whole range; the indictment is the fact.

scenarios.opening_query: There's a warrant out for me from a case I thought was resolved years ago.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Parole/Probation
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's a probation-violation warrant — I moved states and stopped reporting, thinking my term had ended.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's a probation-violation warrant — I moved and stopped reporting, thinking my term was done.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the warrant is a new charge or a supervision violation
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a failure-to-appear warrant on the original misdemeanor — the case itself was never finished.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's failure-to-appear on the original misdemeanor — the case never actually finished.
scenarios.vetting_note: Warrant-type fact separates supervision practice from live defense.
